name: Gmail MCP Server
description: Automated workflow: Gmail MCP Server. This workflow integrates 3 different services: gmailTool, stickyNote, mcpTrigger. It contains 27 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
shortDescription: Gmail MCP Server workflow containing 29 nodes.

originalWorkflowJson.name: Gmail MCP Server

nodes.name: addLabels

parameters.toolDescription: Add one or more labels to an email message. AI-configurable parameters: Message_ID (string) - the ID of the message to label; Label_Names_or_IDs (string) - comma-separated label names or IDs to apply.

nodes.notes: This gmailTool node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: delete

parameters.toolDescription: Delete an email message. AI-configurable parameters: Message_ID (string) - the ID of the message to delete.

nodes.notes: This gmailTool node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: get

parameters.toolDescription: Retrieve details of an email message. AI-configurable parameters: Message_ID (string) - the ID of the message to retrieve.

nodes.notes: This gmailTool node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: search

parameters.toolDescription: Retrieve multiple email messages based on filters. AI-configurable parameters: Return_All (boolean) - whether to return all matching messages; Search (string) - Gmail query string to filter messages; Received_After (string) - datetime (RFC3339) after which messages are received; Received_Before (string) - datetime before which messages are received; Sender (string) - email address of the sender.

nodes.notes: This gmailTool node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: markAsRead

parameters.toolDescription: Mark an email message as read. AI-configurable parameters: Message_ID (string) - the ID of the message to mark as read.

nodes.notes: This gmailTool node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: markAsUnread

parameters.toolDescription: Mark an email message as unread. AI-configurable parameters: Message_ID (string) - the ID of the message to mark as unread.

nodes.notes: This gmailTool node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: reply

parameters.toolDescription: Reply to an email message. AI-configurable parameters: Message_ID (string) - the ID of the message; Message (string) - the reply content; Attachment_Field_Name (string) - input field name containing attachments; BCC (string) - comma-separated BCC recipients; CC (string) - comma-separated CC recipients.

nodes.notes: This gmailTool node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.toolDescription: Remove one or more labels from an email message. AI-configurable parameters: Message_ID (string) - the ID of the message; Label_Names_or_IDs (string) - comma-separated label names or IDs to remove.

nodes.notes: This gmailTool node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.content:
## USAGE

Open the Gmail MCP Server node to obtain the SSE server URL.

Use that to configure an N8N AI Agent flow or other AI tool.

parameters.content:
## USAGE

Open the Gmail MCP Server node to obtain the SSE server URL.

Use that to configure an N8N AI Agent flow or other AI tool.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Manual Trigger".
Open the "addLabels" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "delete" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "get" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "search" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "markAsRead" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "markAsUnread" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "reply" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "removeLabels" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "getLabels" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "getLabel" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "deleteLabel" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "createLabel" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "deleteDraft" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "createDraft" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "getDraft" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "getManyDrafts" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "getManyThreads" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "getThread" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "addLabelThread" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "removeLabelThread" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "replyThread" node (n8n-nodes-base.gmailTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Gmail MCP Server" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
